annotations.label: Principal component analysis (PCA)

anchor.snippet: Principal component analysis ( PCA ) is a linear dimensionality reduction technique

annotations.note: No definition of PCA as a dimensionality-reduction procedure appears anywhere in Mathlib4.

annotations.label: Principal components of a point cloud

anchor.snippet: The principal components of a collection of points in a real coordinate space are a sequence

annotations.note: No notion of principal components of a point cloud is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Best-fitting line

anchor.snippet: a best-fitting line is defined as one that minimizes the average squared perpendicular distance

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of least-squares best-fitting affine subspaces.

annotations.label: First and k-th principal components

anchor.snippet: the first principal component of a set of

annotations.note: No definition of first or k-th principal components is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Principal components are eigenvectors of the covariance matrix

anchor.snippet: it can be shown that the principal components are eigenvectors of the data's covariance matrix

annotations.note: The general spectral theorem for Hermitian matrices is available, but its specialization to a sample covariance matrix interpreted as PCA is not formalized.

annotations.label: PCA as orthogonal linear transformation

anchor.snippet: PCA is defined as an orthogonal linear transformation on a real inner product space

annotations.note: No formal definition of PCA as an orthogonal change-of-basis exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximum of Rayleigh quotient equals largest eigenvalue

anchor.snippet: A standard result for a positive semidefinite matrix such as X T X is that the quotient's maximum possible value is the largest eigenvalue

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the supremum of the Rayleigh quotient of a self-adjoint operator on a finite-dimensional inner product space is attained as its largest eigenvalue.

annotations.label: First principal component score

anchor.snippet: the first principal component of a data vector x ( i ) can then be given as a score

annotations.note: PCA scores are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-th principal component via deflation

anchor.snippet: The k -th component can be found by subtracting the first k − 1 principal components

annotations.note: The deflation construction of successive principal components is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weight vectors are eigenvectors of X^T X

anchor.snippet: It turns out that this gives the remaining eigenvectors of X T X

annotations.note: The general spectral decomposition of Hermitian matrices is in Mathlib, but the identification of PCA weight vectors with eigenvectors of XᵀX is not.

annotations.label: Loadings in PCA

anchor.snippet: Columns of W multiplied by the square root of corresponding eigenvalues

annotations.note: PCA loadings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zero sample covariance between different principal components

anchor.snippet: there is no sample covariance between different principal components over the dataset

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state uncorrelatedness of principal components.

annotations.label: Principal components transformation diagonalises empirical covariance matrix

anchor.snippet: Another way to characterise the principal components transformation is therefore as the transformation to coordinates which diagonalise the empirical sample covariance matrix

annotations.note: The spectral theorem provides orthogonal diagonalization of Hermitian matrices, but no statement about empirical covariance matrices appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eigenvalue equals sum of squared component scores

anchor.snippet: is equal to the sum of the squares over the dataset associated with each component

annotations.note: No statement equating the k-th eigenvalue of XᵀX with the sum of squared k-th principal-component scores appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Singular value decomposition of X

anchor.snippet: the singular value decomposition (SVD) of X

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the singular values of a linear map between finite-dimensional inner product spaces, but does not provide the full UΣVᵀ matrix-factorization form of SVD.

annotations.label: Right singular vectors equal eigenvectors of X^T X

anchor.snippet: Comparison with the eigenvector factorization of X T X establishes that the right singular vectors W of X are equivalent to the eigenvectors of X T X

annotations.note: Mathlib shows the squared singular values are the eigenvalues of T*∘T but does not name right singular vectors or identify them with the eigenvectors of XᵀX explicitly.

annotations.label: Score matrix as polar decomposition

anchor.snippet: This form is also the polar decomposition of T

annotations.note: Mathlib has no matrix polar decomposition lemma identifying the PCA score matrix with the polar form.

annotations.label: Eckart–Young theorem (truncated SVD is best rank-L approximation)

anchor.snippet: produces a truncated matrix that is the nearest possible matrix of rank L to the original matrix

annotations.note: The Eckart–Young low-rank approximation theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Optimal k-dimensional fit (Eckart–Young in PCA form)

anchor.snippet: Theorem (Optimal k‑dimensional fit)

annotations.note: The PCA-formulation of the optimal k-dimensional fit theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sum of eigenvalues equals total squared deviation from mean

anchor.snippet: The sum of all the eigenvalues is equal to the sum of the squared distances of the points from their multidimensional mean

annotations.note: No statement equating the trace of the empirical covariance matrix with total squared deviation from the centroid appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Effect of variable rescaling on covariance matrix

anchor.snippet: PCA is sensitive to the scaling of the variables

annotations.note: Scaling-sensitivity of PCA is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Effect of failing to mean-center

anchor.snippet: Classical PCA assumes the cloud of points has already been translated so its centroid is at the origin

annotations.note: Mathlib has no statement about effects of skipping mean-centering in PCA.

annotations.label: PCA on correlation matrix equals PCA on covariance of standardized data

anchor.snippet: a PCA based on the correlation matrix of X is equal to a PCA based on the covariance matrix of Z

annotations.note: Equivalence of correlation-PCA and standardized-data covariance-PCA is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Property 1 — trace maximised by leading eigenvectors

anchor.snippet: Property 1

annotations.note: The Ky Fan-style trace-maximization characterization of PCA is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Property 2 — trace minimised by trailing eigenvectors

anchor.snippet: Property 2

annotations.note: The dual trace-minimization property of trailing eigenvectors is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Property 3 — spectral decomposition of Σ

anchor.snippet: Property 3

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the spectral decomposition of Hermitian matrices, which subsumes the spectral decomposition of a covariance matrix Σ.

annotations.label: Information-theoretic optimality of PCA (Linsker)

anchor.snippet: Linsker showed that if

annotations.note: Linsker's information-maximization result for PCA is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: PCA minimises upper bound on information loss for non-Gaussian signal

anchor.snippet: PCA at least minimizes an upper bound on the information loss

annotations.note: No information-loss upper bound for PCA appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Karhunen–Loève transform via covariance method

anchor.snippet: where Y is the Karhunen–Loève transform (KLT) of matrix X

annotations.note: Grep finds no occurrences of Karhunen–Loève in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of orthonormal P diagonalising cov(X)

anchor.snippet: cov( X ) is guaranteed to be a non-negative definite matrix and thus is guaranteed to be diagonalisable by some unitary matrix

annotations.note: Mathlib's Hermitian spectral theorem gives unitary diagonalization, generalizing the statement for non-negative-definite covariance matrices.

annotations.label: Power iteration converges to leading principal component

anchor.snippet: the vector r gets close to the first principal component of X within the number of iterations

annotations.note: Mathlib has no convergence theorem for the power-iteration algorithm applied to XᵀX.

annotations.label: Two correlated variables — 45° rotation under PCA

anchor.snippet: If we have just two variables and they have the same sample variance and are completely correlated, then the PCA will entail a rotation by 45°

annotations.note: This worked example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Oxford Internet Survey four attitude components

anchor.section: Market research and indexes of attitude
anchor.snippet: the Oxford Internet Survey in 2013 asked 2000 people about their attitudes

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no empirical-research applications of PCA.

annotations.label: Spike-triggered covariance analysis in neuroscience

anchor.snippet: This technique is known as spike-triggered covariance analysis

annotations.note: Spike-triggered covariance analysis is not formalized in Mathlib.
